question_text: A student consumes a chocolate bar containing 2000 joules of chemical energy. If the student's body converts 40% of this energy into useful work (movement and other bodily functions), calculate the energy lost as heat.
explanation: When energy is transferred in the human body, not all of it becomes useful work - some is always lost as heat. If 40% is converted to useful work, then the remaining 60% is lost as heat. To find 60% of 2000 J: 0.60 × 2000 = 1200 J. This shows that the human body is not 100% efficient at converting chemical energy from food into movement and other functions.
